message:
feat(stdlib): add dehumanize_number (NetBSD size-string parser) (bd-dyrgj)

NetBSD libutil dehumanize_number(str, size) — parse a C string of
optional whitespace + optional sign + decimal digits + optional
one-character size suffix into an int64. Used by NetBSD ftpd,
mtree, dd-conv, and other utilities for human-readable size limits
in config files.

* core::stdlib::dehumanize_number::parse returns
  Result<i64, DehumanizeError> with Invalid / Overflow variants.
  Suffix multipliers: b/B = 1, k/K = 1024, m/M = 1024^2, g/G = 1024^3,
  t/T = 1024^4, p/P = 1024^5, e/E = 1024^6. Rejects decimal points
  (the integer-only cousin of FreeBSD expand_number), trailing
  garbage, embedded whitespace, hex prefixes, and unknown suffixes.
  Detects overflow during accumulation AND the suffix multiply.
  Special-cases i64::MIN so the canonical extreme parses cleanly.
* stdlib_abi::dehumanize_number shim handles NUL-terminated input,
  NULL-pointer guards (returns -1+EINVAL), and maps Invalid →
  EINVAL / Overflow → ERANGE per NetBSD documentation.

28 core unit tests (every suffix, every error class, i64 boundary
cases) + 10 abi integration tests (errno mapping, NULL safety,
all 14 suffix spellings, leading-whitespace acceptance). Clippy-
clean, fmt-clean. All builds via rch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
